title: urtica praetermissa subsp. praetermissa
intro: Urtica praetermissa subsp. praetermissa, also known as the lesser nettle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the nettle family Urticaceae, characterized by its nettle-like leaves and small, greenish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Urtica praetermissa subsp. praetermissa include its ability to alleviate symptoms of allergies, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for a variety of uses in natural remedies and holistic health practices.
